description: Candidate sequence patterns for witness detection. These are observed and surfaced, never enforced.
design_note: The witness describes patterns. It does not judge them. Each pattern is a factual description of a sequence shape. The human decides what it means.

patterns.name: credential-adjacent-egress
patterns.description: Sensitive file read followed by outbound network request
patterns.fact_description: Sensitive file accessed at {step1.timestamp}, followed by outbound network request at {step2.timestamp} ({delta}s apart)
patterns.not_a_conclusion: This is not labeled as exfiltration. It is a sequence of facts. The human interprets.

patterns.name: denied-then-scheduled
patterns.description: Denied request followed by schedule or cron creation
patterns.fact_description: Request denied at {step1.timestamp} (rule {step1.rule}), followed by schedule creation at {step2.timestamp} ({delta}s apart)
patterns.not_a_conclusion: This is not labeled as evasion. It is a sequence of facts. The human interprets.

patterns.name: config-behavior-shift
patterns.description: Config file modification followed by change in tool usage or approval pattern

steps.note: Step 2 requires comparing post-config behavior against pre-config baseline. This is a future capability — for now, flag the config write itself as notable.

patterns.not_a_conclusion: Config modification is noted. Behavioral change detection is not yet implemented. The human reviews.

patterns.name: memory-justified-action
patterns.description: Memory or continuity file write followed by action that may reference that memory

steps.note: Step 2 requires detecting whether a later action references the memory entry. This is a future capability — for now, flag the memory write as notable if it contains permission-like language.

patterns.not_a_conclusion: Memory writes are noted. Reference detection is not yet implemented. The human reviews.

patterns.name: rapid-approval-drift
patterns.description: Approval latency decreasing significantly over time within a session
patterns.fact_description: Approval latency decreased from {step1.latency}ms to {step2.latency}ms over session duration
patterns.not_a_conclusion: This is not labeled as rubber-stamping. It is a measurement. The human interprets.

patterns.name: repeated-denial-burst
patterns.description: Same rule denies 5+ times in a single session
patterns.fact_description: {count} denials of rule {rule} in session {session}, from {first_ts} to {last_ts}
patterns.not_a_conclusion: Repeated denials may indicate the agent is probing boundaries, or the human is asking it to do something the policy prevents. This is a pattern worth review, not evidence of malice. The human interprets.
